reviews.review_description:
Update: Finished. Really good one! Would encourage the developer to continue with this. Some scenes and effects look even better than in Eclipse Edge of Light, and it is immersive and fun to play. Like the elevating platforms a lot, this is great in VR!

 Good game, it seems the dev is a big fan of Eclipse Edge of Light as the scenes are very similar. But it is fun to play nevertheless.

developer_response.body: thanks again for the feedback. I'm planning to expand the game with some more levels, even a new episode and some experiments...

reviews.review_description:
Great retro style and fun to play. with the puzzles just right to make for an enjoyable game without giving you a headache.
Controls work well in view of the Go controller limitations.
Definitely recommended.
Good Game for the price. You will panic in some levels. Caution: scary enemys. Totally recomendet.
Yes, this kind of simple, eerie, yet beautiful atmosphere, we need more fps and exploratory game like this on go. This game is so intriguing. Go get this game now.

This is the type of game I have been waiting for the Go. Great atmosphere, puzzles and shooting mechanics here. For this low price Wrong Voyage Ex is an outstanding deal. It just keeps getting better and better. I am really enjoying it.

Great atmosphere and fun
100% recommendable.

First of all congrats to the developer of creating such an addictive game ! When I started playing it I could not stop ! Also kudos for adding new content ! To the game
Itself now the graphics remind me quite a lot of my youth ( doom ,Wolfenstein etc ) , the developer is using them very wisely by creating a very atmospheric game ! Imagine a combination of a adventure game with some horror and fps elements ! The controls for shooting is a bit difficult ( a crosshairs would help ) and this is the only reason I don’t give it 5
A mix of puzzle adventure with terror. Good idea. Simple graphics but very well made. Of course it make you want more at the end, but it's well worth the price.
One of best games I have played on  the go and I have played quite a few
Excellent gentle puzzle explorer, with lots of content and very reasonably priced, with extra bonus levels appearing!
It has puzzles, different environments, and enemies. For the wonderfully low price that it is a must buy for any gaming fan that owns an Oculus go.

Great atmosphere with pixelated textures as a stylistic. Different settings and gameplay variations. 

For 1,99 it's a steal!

of Art! Great immersive atmosphere with simple but beautiful graphics. 

Controls are good but would much better if you could walk backwards. Could you please add a possibilty to walk backwards, for example with gamepad support?

developer_response.body: thank you very much for the feedback. About the gamepad support, i tried to implement it before launch, but had some bugs playing with the Go controller at the same time. I'll try it again but i can't promise anything. Anyway at this moment you can move backwards pointing with the go controller to your back, although i know it's not very confortable playing seated. Hope i can find a better solution. Thanks again for comment.

reviews.review_description: everything is simple and effective, nice graphics, controls are solid, tilt takes u sideways, remember... best to play standing, watch for sneaky critters around u, a little scary but no heart attacks haha, a nice mix of skill and practice, and a gun target would be nice, but not too bad ... nice job!
Game is great.  Soundtrack is very good. Some of the monsters rushing me scare the jebeezus out of me.  Thank you.

A nice pick up and play game with short tight levels, not sure how many there are as I’ve only completed the first few stages. Resembles Doom in the dark corridor stages in play and in tone. Bought it on sale, so for 79p it’s a no brainier, recommended.

Wrong Voyage shares similarities with Eclipse, Lands End and Althora. 
I really like these immersive adventure games but this one differs from the other three in that there are a variety of unsettling creatures that need to be dispatched, this gives it a level of atmospheric tension without it becoming to distracting. The environments are nicely done and very spacious with simple but lovely graphics. There is a bit of puzzle solving as you move through but nothing to difficult.
An enjoyable jaunt through virtual reality and a bargain at around $1.50

reviews.review_title: This is among the best VR games
reviews.review_description: Please make more games! You definitely have the skill and passion!

I bought this on sale for 49p but i would have happily payed full price. Easily in my top 5 oculus go games, not too easy, very immersive with a few scares thrown in. Really pushes the go to its limits. Well done everybody involved

Billed as a FPS/exploration/puzzle hybrid, this game certainly delivers on all those fronts while somehow being more than merely the sum of those parts. In many ways, this title theoretically should not have worked: it heavily borrows so many elements from other games, it has many stylistic similarities to other titles, sometimes it’s not sure what it wants to be, and the graphics are not exactly cutting edge. Yet, all of those supposed weaknesses are somehow also its strengths. In regards to emulating other games, the premise and tone are so familiar that many people have dubbed it “Eclipse: Edge Of Light with a gun.” This generalization is understandable, but a generalization nonetheless. I did not feel it to be merely a retread, but an evolution, much like how Eclipse seemed to evolve from the very basic concepts of Land’s End and Daedalus. I would modify the comparison to be “imagine Eclipse with a 90’s Doom-like element added, minus the epic grandeur.” In addressing the dissonant hybrid style, it just works. Like how a recipe calls for various ingredients that somehow mix and add up to a fine dish. It can’t be merely described but experienced. For the cheap price tag, this experience is a must. I thoroughly enjoyed it, but not only that, I was thoroughly surprised that I enjoyed it so much. For content-to-dollar ratio, it’s right up there with the best value of anything on the platform.
